Title: OXIDATIVE SIGNAL-INDUCIBLE1 induces immunity by coordinating N-hydroxypipecolic acid, salicylic acid, and camalexin synthesis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Respiratory burst oxidase homolog; NADPH oxidase
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; DAB staining for H2O2 accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RBOHD transcripts are elevated in OXI1 overexpression lines. 2. OXI1-induced ROS accumulation correlates with increased RBOHD/RBOHF expression. 3. This supports a role for OXI1 in ROS biosynthesis and homeostasis during immunity.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ROS accumulation downstream of OXI1
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as ROS-generation gene rather than nutrient metabolite biosynthetic enzyme
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray: CATdb Project AU2010-01_OXI1; GEO GSE35941
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native OXI1 promoter (pOXI1) in OXI1 constructs
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ROS overaccumulation associated with cell death
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: ROS pathway modulation could strengthen defense but has high risk of lesion and growth penalties

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Respiratory burst oxidase homolog; NADPH oxidase
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RBOHF expression is increased in OXI1 gain-of-function lines. 2. Increased RBOHF accompanies elevated H2O2 accumulation. 3. RBOHF forms part of the OXI1-controlled ROS module in immunity.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ROS accumulation downstream of OXI1
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Table specifically lists ATRBOHF upregulated in OE2
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray: CATdb Project AU2010-01_OXI1; GEO GSE35941
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native OXI1 promoter (pOXI1) in OXI1 constructs
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ROS overaccumulation associated with cell death

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: AGD2-like defense response protein 1 / lysine aminotransferase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Systemic acquired resistance and enhanced pathogen immunity
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts L-lysine to 2,3-dehydropipecolic acid in the lysine-derived pipecolate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for Pip/NHP biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Pathogen inducible; strongly upregulated in OXI1 overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Elevated in 4-wk-old OE2 plants; induced upon pathogen attack
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae pv maculicola infection; OXI1 overexpression background
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling of Pip/NHP; genetic cross with OE2 ald1; pathogen resistance assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ALD1 transcripts are upregulated in OXI1 overexpression plants. 2. ALD1 is required for constitutive Pip and NHP accumulation in OE2. 3. Loss of ALD1 suppresses the enhanced pathogen resistance of OXI1-OE, showing ALD1-mediated NHP pathway is essential for OXI1 immunity output.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes Pip and downstream NHP accumulation and is required for OXI1-mediated enhanced immunity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Constitutive Pip accumulation in OE2 is completely suppressed in OE2 ald1; contributes to positive feedback in NHP pathway
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray: CATdb Project AU2010-01_OXI1; GEO GSE35941; Metabolomics: MassIVE MSV000090447; Proteomics: PRIDE PXD023853
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native OXI1 promoter (pOXI1) in OXI1 overexpression/complementation constructs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: infected local leaves and distal systemic leaves
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated with OXI1 overexpression context causing growth reduction and cell death
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stacking target with OXI1/FMO1 to enhance disease-associated NHP signaling, but constitutive activation may cause growth-defense penalties

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Systemic acquired resistance and enhanced pathogen immunity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Pathogen inducible; strongly upregulated in OXI1 overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae pv maculicola infection; OXI1 overexpression background
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SARD4 transcripts are elevated in OXI1 overexpression lines. 2. OXI1 promotes Pip/NHP biosynthesis through transcriptional upregulation of SARD4 together with ALD1 and FMO1. 3. Increased SARD4 expression correlates with constitutive and primed Pip/NHP accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes Pip production and thereby supports downstream NHP accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as part of the OXI1-induced Pip/NHP module, but no direct mutant test in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray: CATdb Project AU2010-01_OXI1; GEO GSE35941; Metabolomics: MassIVE MSV000090447; Proteomics: PRIDE PXD023853
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native OXI1 promoter (pOXI1) in OXI1 constructs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: infected local leaves and distal systemic leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering elevated defense metabolite production via Pip/NHP pathway, likely best used in controlled or inducible combinations

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Systemic acquired resistance and enhanced pathogen immunity
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates pipecolic acid to N-hydroxypipecolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Pathogen inducible; elevated in OXI1 overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Elevated in 4-wk-old OE2 plants; induced during infection
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae pv maculicola infection; OXI1 overexpression background
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; NHP metabolite profiling; genetic cross with OE2 fmo1; pathogen resistance assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FMO1 transcripts are increased in OXI1 overexpression plants. 2. FMO1 is essential for constitutive NHP accumulation and OXI1-mediated enhanced resistance. 3. fmo1 suppresses the OXI1-OE gain-of-function phenotypes and attenuates Pip accumulation, supporting NHP-dependent positive feedback.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly drives NHP accumulation and is essential for OXI1-mediated immunity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OE2 fmo1 fully suppresses constitutive NHP accumulation and the strong resistance phenotype; also attenuates Pip, consistent with positive feedback
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray: CATdb Project AU2010-01_OXI1; GEO GSE35941; Metabolomics: MassIVE MSV000090447; Proteomics: PRIDE PXD023853
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native OXI1 promoter (pOXI1) in OXI1 constructs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: infected local leaves and distal systemic leaves
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In OXI1-OE context associated with growth reduction/cell death
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for immune priming through NHP engineering, but constitutive activation may impose defense-growth costs

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Immune signaling, programmed cell death, pathogen resistance
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts chorismate to isochorismate in the SA biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms precursor pool for target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for SA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly upregulated in OXI1 overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Elevated in 4-wk-old OE2 plants; induced by infection
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Psm infection; OXI1 overexpression; NHP pathway status affects constitutive SA output
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: microarray; qRT-PCR; SA/SAG/SGE metabolite profiling; genetic discussion using sid2 cross
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SID2 expression is elevated in OXI1-overexpressing plants. 2. Elevated SID2 correlates with constitutive SA, SAG, and SGE accumulation. 3. OXI1-induced SA accumulation is partly dependent on the NHP pathway and SID2-linked SA biosynthesis contributes to OXI1 cell death phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SA accumulation downstream of OXI1-regulated transcriptional network
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion notes OE2 sid2 partially suppresses dwarf/cell death phenotype based on prior work; SA induction under pathogen challenge also includes an NHP-independent branch
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray: CATdb Project AU2010-01_OXI1; GEO GSE35941; Metabolomics: MassIVE MSV000090447; Proteomics: PRIDE PXD023853
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native OXI1 promoter (pOXI1) in OXI1 constructs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High SA associated with cell death and growth reduction in OXI1-OE context
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for immune enhancement but likely requires inducible control to avoid constitutive SA-associated penalties

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial defense metabolite for pathogen resistance
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a key cytochrome P450 step required for camalexin formation from indolic precursors
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly upregulated in OXI1 overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Elevated in 4-wk-old OE2 plants; associated with pathogen-induced accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Psm infection; OXI1 overexpression; partially influenced by NHP pathway under mock conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAD3 transcripts are strongly increased in OXI1 overexpression plants. 2. Elevated PAD3 correlates with constitutive and enhanced pathogen-induced camalexin accumulation. 3. OXI1-triggered camalexin accumulation is partially reduced in OE2 ald1 and OE2 fmo1 under noninfected conditions, linking PAD3-associated output to the NHP pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes camalexin accumulation downstream of OXI1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as WRKY33 target in OXI1-mediated camalexin induction
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray: CATdb Project AU2010-01_OXI1; GEO GSE35941; Metabolomics: MassIVE MSV000090447; Proteomics: PRIDE PXD023853
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native OXI1 promoter (pOXI1) in OXI1 constructs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Part of strong defense activation associated with cell death in OXI1-OE context
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for enhanced phytoalexin-based resistance, especially with upstream regulators, but constitutive activation may be costly

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial defense metabolite for pathogen resistance
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts tryptophan to indole-3-acetaldoxime, supplying precursor for camalexin and related indolic defense metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly upregulated in OXI1 overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: microarray/transcript profiling; camalexin metabolite profiling correlation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP79B2 transcripts are strongly elevated in OXI1-overexpressing plants. 2. OXI1 activates the indolic defense pathway by increasing IAOx precursor supply. 3. This correlates with increased camalexin accumulation in OE2 plants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances precursor flux into camalexin/indolic defense metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also supports related indolic products such as 4-OH-ICN branch
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray: CATdb Project AU2010-01_OXI1; GEO GSE35941
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native OXI1 promoter (pOXI1) in OXI1 constructs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May reroute tryptophan metabolism toward defense
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential precursor-supply engineering target for indolic defense metabolites

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress and defense-associated polyamine metabolism; possible contribution to ROS-related responses
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates arginine to agmatine in the putrescine biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; global metabolomics for arginine and putrescine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ADC gene expression is upregulated in OXI1 overexpressors. 2. OXI1-OE plants accumulate arginine and putrescine. 3. OXI1 therefore induces the early polyamine biosynthetic module, although spermidine branch genes are downregulated.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes putrescine-associated polyamine accumulation in OXI1-OE plants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper reports ADC1 and ADC2 expression analysis; polyamine branch does not proceed to spermidine accumulation in OXI1-OE
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics: MassIVE MSV000090447; Microarray: CATdb Project AU2010-01_OXI1; GEO GSE35941
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native OXI1 promoter (pOXI1) in OXI1 constructs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Polyamine shifts may alter growth-defense balance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary candidate for defense-associated polyamine modulation, but pathway output appears partial in this study

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Serine/threonine protein kinase; AGC-like oxidative signal-inducible kinase
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: amino acid-derived defense metabolite; phenolic defense hormone; indolic phytoalexin
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pathogen resistance, systemic acquired resistance, immune amplification, programmed cell death
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Defense metabolite biosynthesis, ROS generation/homeostasis, immune priming
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive regulation of transcriptional programs leading to metabolite biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Stress conditions, reactive oxygen species, H2O2, cellulose, pathogen attack
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central positive regulator coordinating NHP, SA, and camalexin synthesis; OXI1-OE confers constitutive accumulation and priming, while knockout increases susceptibility
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by multiple abiotic and biotic stresses; strong expression along vascular tissue
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strong in vascular system; overexpression lines with high OXI1 protein show GOF cell death and defense activation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: knockout and genomic overexpression/complementation lines; transcriptomics; proteomics; metabolomics; qRT-PCR; pathogen assays; genetic crosses with ald1 and fmo1
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ALD1; SARD4; FMO1; CBP60g; SARD1; SID2; WRKY33; PAD3; RBOHD; RBOHF; CYP79B2; CYP82C2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of Pip/NHP, SA, camalexin, and ROS-associated genes
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ambient growth; pathogen infection with Psm, Pst DC3000, Pst AvrRpm1, Hpa Emwa1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes constitutive and primed accumulation of NHP, SA, and camalexin, enhances pathogen resistance, and triggers ROS and cell death when overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Acts upstream of NHP pathway that exhibits positive feedback on Pip/NHP accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OXI1 overexpression induces ALD1, SARD4, and FMO1 to drive Pip and NHP biosynthesis. 2. OXI1 also promotes SA biosynthesis via CBP60g/SARD1-dependent SID2 induction and camalexin accumulation via WRKY33/PAD3. 3. Enhanced resistance and cell death phenotypes of OXI1-OE are abolished in ald1 or fmo1 backgrounds, showing that the NHP pathway is essential for OXI1-mediated immunity.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GOF phenotypes include reduced growth, ROS accumulation, and late developmental leaf cell death; photosynthesis genes/proteins are repressed in overexpressors and elevated in oxi1 mutants
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray: CATdb Project AU2010-01_OXI1; GEO GSE35941; Proteomics: PRIDE PXD023853; Metabolomics: MassIVE MSV000090447
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native OXI1 promoter (pOXI1) genomic OXI1 overexpression/complementation construct
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: local infected leaves and distal systemic leaves
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive activation causes dwarfing/slight reduced growth, ROS accumulation, cell death, and repression of photosynthesis-related genes/proteins
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong immune-engineering hub, but use likely requires inducible/tissue-specific tuning to avoid severe growth and cell-death penalties

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Redundantly regulates SID2 expression, linking OXI1 to SA synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: microarray; qRT-PCR correlation with SA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SA accumulation downstream of OXI1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CBP60g is transcriptionally induced in OXI1 overexpression plants. 2. As a known regulator of SID2, CBP60g provides a mechanistic link between OXI1 and enhanced SA biosynthesis. 3. Elevated CBP60g expression correlates with constitutive SA, SAG, and SGE accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper states CBP60g and SARD1 redundantly regulate SID2
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray: CATdb Project AU2010-01_OXI1; GEO GSE35941
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native OXI1 promoter (pOXI1) in OXI1 constructs
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Part of constitutive defense activation network
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate upstream regulator for SA-mediated resistance, but likely needs dosage control

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Redundant key activator of SID2 expression in OXI1-mediated SA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: microarray; qRT-PCR correlation with SA profiling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SA accumulation downstream of OXI1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SARD1 is upregulated in OXI1 overexpression lines. 2. Together with CBP60g, SARD1 links OXI1 to elevated SID2 expression. 3. This correlates with constitutive SA accumulation and primed SA responses.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also historically linked with systemic acquired resistance signaling
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray: CATdb Project AU2010-01_OXI1; GEO GSE35941
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native OXI1 promoter (pOXI1) in OXI1 constructs
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Part of constitutive defense activation network
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible SA-priming target, preferably in inducible designs

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct upstream regulator linking OXI1 to PAD3-dependent camalexin synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly upregulated in OXI1 overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: microarray/transcript profiling; camalexin metabolite profiling correlation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: OXI1 overexpression; pathogen challenge with Psm
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes constitutive and enhanced induced camalexin accumulation downstream of OXI1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRKY33 transcripts are increased in OXI1 overexpression plants. 2. OXI1-mediated camalexin induction is associated with WRKY33-targeted PAD3 transcription. 3. Elevated WRKY33 correlates with higher basal and pathogen-induced camalexin levels.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper explicitly frames camalexin accumulation as WRKY33-targeted transcription of PAD3
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray: CATdb Project AU2010-01_OXI1; GEO GSE35941
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native OXI1 promoter (pOXI1) in OXI1 constructs
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could intensify defense-associated growth costs when constitutive
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for pathogen-induced phytoalexin enhancement
